nodes.label: Fun with Symmetry
nodes.description: Understanding symmetry, mirror images, and symmetric figures

nodes.label: What is Symmetry?
nodes.description: When one half of a shape mirrors the other half exactly

nodes.label: Line of Symmetry
nodes.description: The line that divides a shape into two matching halves

nodes.label: One or Many Lines
nodes.description: Some shapes have 1, 2, 3, 4 or even unlimited lines of symmetry

nodes.label: Mirror Images
nodes.description: Reflections where left and right sides are swapped

nodes.label: Mirror Line
nodes.description: Place a mirror on the line of symmetry to see the complete shape

nodes.label: Reflections
nodes.description: The image flips across the line - like a lake reflecting a tree

nodes.label: Symmetric Shapes
nodes.description: Shapes and objects that have symmetry

nodes.label: Common Shapes
nodes.description: Square (4 lines), rectangle (2 lines), circle (unlimited lines)

nodes.label: Letters & Objects
nodes.description: Letters like A, M, O, H and objects like kites, rangoli have symmetry

nodes.label: Folding and Cutting
nodes.description: Fold paper and cut to make symmetric shapes

nodes.label: Fold Test
nodes.description: Fold along a line - if both halves match, it is a line of symmetry

nodes.label: Paper Cutting
nodes.description: Fold paper, draw half a shape, cut and open for a symmetric figure

nodes.label: Symmetry in Nature
nodes.description: Symmetry seen in butterflies, leaves, flowers, and the human body

nodes.label: Nature Examples
nodes.description: Butterfly wings, leaf veins, flower petals, starfish arms

nodes.label: Completing Figures
nodes.description: Draw the mirror half to complete a symmetric figure
